0.title: Keychron's Q Ultra ZMK series brings 660h battery to the prebuilt-premium tier
0.lede: Keychron used CES 2026 to announce the Q Ultra line — Q1 through Q6 prebuilts running ZMK rather than QMK/VIA. The 660h battery claim is the headline; the firmware swap is the actual story.

1.title: When customs became vendor-first
1.lede: From 2018 through 2022 the typical custom shipped through one host vendor and a fan of regional proxies; by 2026 the baseline custom is a configurator on a vendor's own storefront. Boards moved. Keycap group buys mostly didn't.

2.title: What Cherry's MX2A revision actually changed
2.lede: Cherry's MX2A landed in 2023 with a real engineering update — factory lube, tighter tolerances, the same 100M-press lifespan. The reason the discourse cooled isn't that the switch failed; it's that the enthusiast slot it aimed at was already claimed by the time it arrived.

3.title: GMK CYL Prussian Alert opens at KBDfans on May 15
3.lede: GMK CYL Prussian Alert lands at KBDfans on 2026-05-15 with a Bauhaus-leaning red-on-cream palette. The IC has been the loudest GMK CYL thread on GeekHack heading into the buy.

4.title: The split/ergo cohort grew up
4.lede: Split and ergonomic boards spent a decade as a hobbyist sub-cohort orbiting a stagger-majority. By 2026 the Voyager line, the Glove80, and a steady trickle of r/mk transition posts have pushed split/ergo across a visibility threshold — still a minority, but now a reference category instead of a footnote.

5.title: How 75 percent became the default custom layout
5.lede: Two years ago a custom-board recommendation defaulted to 65% with a polite note about 75% as the next step up. The Sonnet 2026 refresh, the QK75, and a crowded mid-tier have flipped that order. 75% is now the layout customs reach for first.

6.title: GMK CYL Ishtar R2 closes at KBDfans with a second-and-likely-last shot at the lapis Mesopotamia palette
6.lede: GMK CYL Ishtar R2 ran 2026-04-17 through 2026-05-10 at KBDfans. The editorial event is not the open — it is the close. A second round on a Cherry-profile doubleshot ABS set is the second-and-likely-last shot at MSRP before the aftermarket takes over the palette.

7.title: How HMX took over the mid-tier linear slot
7.lede: Two years ago HMX was a name on a vendor page nobody pronounced confidently. Today the Cloud sits second on our W19 tracker and lands in half the $120 prebuilts the scene argues about. The rise wasn't an accident.

8.title: Lubing 101: what matters, what doesn't, and where to stop
8.lede: Lube moves the sound and feel of a switch in ways nothing else in the build can. It also chews up a Saturday and ruins switches when applied with the enthusiasm the rabbit-hole crowd recommends. Here is the version that earns its keep.

9.title: Why typing tests lie
9.lede: Monkeytype scores reward fast and forgiving. A daily driver needs comfortable and honest. Optimizing a build for the test selects for the wrong things — and the gap shows up around hour two, not in the leaderboard.

10.title: GSK Sweet Nightmare runs a four-day window at KBDfans with an eight-variant matrix
10.lede: Goldenstar's Sweet Nightmare resin run opened at KBDfans on 2026-05-08 and closes 2026-05-11. The four-day window is unusually tight for a formal group buy, and the eight-variant matrix — four colorways across two finishes — compresses a configurator's worth of choices into a half-week.

11.title: Reading the Trends Tracker
11.lede: thock's Trends Tracker is a weekly snapshot of what is rising, falling, or holding flat across the hobby — switches, keycaps, layouts, vendors, and brands, scored on a single -100 to 100 scale and updated every Friday. This is what the columns mean and what they do not.

12.title: Pairing Oil Kings with the Mode Sonnet
12.lede: This pairing has been on the bench for months, and a free Sunday finally made it happen. The Sonnet is a soft 65 with a top-mount-leaf gasket, and Oil Kings are a heavy factory-lubed linear — together they hit a register worth coming back to.

13.title: Mode Sonnet R2 opens at CannonKeys
13.lede: Mode has announced the second round of its 65% Sonnet board at CannonKeys, now scheduled for 2026-06-01 through 2026-07-15 after a vendor-side timeline shift. See the in-article update for the new dates and accent-weight color options.

14.title: The slow fade of Alice layouts
14.lede: Alice mounts had a clear moment between 2021 and 2023 — the splayed alphas, the angled bar, the promise of a typing posture that didn't twist your wrists. The thock trends snapshot for 2026-W19 pegs Alice movement at -18 and trending down. The layout still has loyal users, but the curve has bent.

15.title: Why the Gateron Oil King sounds the way it does
15.lede: Few stock linears earn the word 'creamy' on first press. The Oil King does, and the reason is not a secret stem material or a proprietary lube — it is a careful pairing of housing resins and a factory tune that other linears under thirty cents tend to skip.

16.title: GMK CYL GREG returns for round 2 at KBDfans
16.lede: Round 2 of GMK CYL GREG opened at KBDfans on 2026-05-01 and runs through 2026-05-29. The four-week window sits on the longer end for GMK CYL R2s, and the round-2 cadence itself is becoming the more interesting story as sets prove out in R1 and accumulate latent demand.

17.title: A beginner's guide to picking your first switch
17.lede: Switches are the single biggest factor in how a keyboard feels and sounds, and the catalog has grown overwhelming. This guide narrows the entire market into three families, explains who each one is for, and gives a three-step path from curiosity to a confident first purchase. By the end, the reader can pick a category without second-guessing.

18.title: GMK CYL King of the Seas opens at KBDfans in two colorways at once
18.lede: MiKeyDigit's nautical doubleshot ABS set opened at KBDfans on 2026-04-28 and runs through 2026-05-31. The rarer angle is the two-colorway split — The Sovereign in deep ocean blues and The Mutation in electric pink, sold concurrently inside the same buy rather than staggered as separate runs.

19.title: How 'thock' became an acoustic spec, not an accident
19.lede: A decade ago a thocky board was the win condition of a $300 custom — the payoff for the right switches, the right lube job, the right patience. In 2026 the same sound is table-stakes for a $150 prebuilt: factory-lubed switches, silicone dampening, gasket-mounted plates, PBT doubleshot. The stack that used to be enthusiast-only is now what mid-tier vendors design toward from day one.

20.title: How Hall-effect quietly became the new mid-premium default
20.lede: The polling-rate hype is loud and easy to wave off — 8K, 32K, numbers chasing each other up the spec sheet. The quieter story is that the magnetic-switch tier has stopped being a Wooting-shaped niche and has settled into the $100–$230 prebuilt slot the scene actually buys from. The volume has moved, not just the marketing.

21.title: The Nyawice puts a leaf-spring F1 mount under a friction-fit-cat-ears Alice
21.lede: An Alice-layout board with a leaf-spring F1 mount, a 9-degree typing angle, and four colorways of friction-fit silicone cat ears is open at CannonKeys through 2026-05-17. The mount type is the editorial story; the cat ears are the conversation.

22.title: ZMK is becoming the wireless default for premium prebuilts
22.lede: A five-board flagship lineup running ZMK isn't a niche move; it's a market signal that an open-source firmware that lived in DIY-wireless territory for half a decade has crossed into mainstream-prebuilt anchor status. The vendor side has finally caught up with where the cohort already was.
